commit.message:
perf: use per-face Hausdorff deflection in BVHLowerBoundDistance (#278)

* perf: use per-face Hausdorff deflection in BVHLowerBoundDistance

Previously, BVHLowerBoundDistance subtracted fBVHDeflection (=
kRelativeDeflection × maxFaceDiag, the global maximum over all faces)
from the BVH mesh distance to guarantee a conservative lower bound.
For multi-face solids where the largest face dominates fBVHDeflection
but the nearest triangle is on a smaller face, this over-subtracted the
correction and produced a looser lower bound than necessary, causing more
points to fall through to the expensive exact DistanceToOut solver.

Introduce fFaceDeflections: a per-face table mapping each face in
fFaceBoundsCache to kRelativeDeflection × face_bbox_diagonal.  After
the BVH traversal, use BRepExtrema_TriangleSet::GetFaceID(bestIdx) to
map the BVH-reordered triangle index back to its originating face, then
look up fFaceDeflections[faceId] for the tightest valid correction
instead of the global maximum.

This avoids the naive approach of pre-indexing by triangle: the BVH
build reorders triangles via Swap() (tracked in myTrgIdxMap), so
BestIndex() returns a post-reorder index that does not match a simple
per-triangle table built in face-traversal order.  GetFaceID() resolves
the reordering correctly.

Falls back to fBVHDeflection if BestIndex is unavailable or out of range.
fBVHDeflection is retained for the SurfaceNormal seed distance calculation.

DTO lower-bound violations remain 0 (verified by test suite).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: combine sanitizer and tsan jobs into a matrix job (#289)

Replace the separate `sanitizer` and `tsan` jobs with a single
`sanitizer` matrix job with two entries:
- `asan`: builds with -DUSE_ASAN=ON -DUSE_UBSAN=ON
- `tsan`: builds with -DUSE_TSAN=ON

All four sanitizer env vars (ASAN_OPTIONS, LSAN_OPTIONS, UBSAN_OPTIONS,
TSAN_OPTIONS) are defined unconditionally at job level; inactive
sanitizers simply ignore the unrelated variables.

The B1 multi-threaded run now executes in both matrix entries, providing
thread-safety coverage under both ASAN and TSAN.

The FetchContent cache key now includes the matrix sanitizer name to
avoid cross-entry cache collisions.

Also update AGENTS.md to reflect the new matrix structure and clarify
that tsan.supp is now a permanent suppression file (not conditional).

commit.message:
perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector in Inside() with BVH triangle ray-cast (#277)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector in Inside() with BVH triangle ray-cast

Replace the per-face IntCurvesFace_Intersector calls for curved faces in
G4OCCTSolid::Inside() with a single BVH traversal of the pre-built fTriangleSet
using Moller-Trumbore ray-triangle intersection.

Previously, every curved face invoked IntCurvesFace_Intersector::Perform()
individually. For a TwistedTubs solid with many curved faces, this resulted in
N_curved_faces expensive OCCT solver calls per Inside() query.

The new TriangleRayCast BVH traversal class casts the same +Z ray through the
BVH-accelerated triangle mesh (fTriangleSet) in a single O(log N_triangles)
traversal, counting crossings via Moller-Trumbore. Degenerate hits (ray near
triangle edge/vertex) fall back to BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier as before.
When fTriangleSet is unavailable, the original per-face path is retained as a
fallback.

Observed speedup in test suite wall-clock time: ~430 s -> ~62 s (7x) for the
full geometry validation run including TwistedTubs and ellipsoid fixtures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden TriangleRayCast and Inside() Tier-2a BVH path per review (#284)

* Initial plan

* fix: apply review feedback to TriangleRayCast and Inside() BVH path

- Reset myCrossings/myOnSurface/myDegenerate in SetRay() so each cast
  starts from a clean state (prevents misuse on reuse)
- Replace 1e100/1e-12 sentinels in RejectNode() with Precision::Infinite()
  / Precision::Confusion() per OCCT conventions
- Add zero-crossings fallback to exact BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier in
  Tier-2a: zero crossings can still mean interior if hits were missed
- Add deflection-band guard in Tier-2a: fall back to exact classifier
  when BVHLowerBoundDistance(p) < tolerance + fBVHDeflection to avoid
  tessellation-error misclassification near the boundary

commit.message:
ci: extract RelWithDebInfo build into build-reldebinfo prerequisite job (#290)

* ci: extract RelWithDebInfo build into build-reldebinfo prerequisite job

The three callgrind profile jobs each ran a full CMake configure+build
before profiling. The matrix job rebuilt the same bench_callgrind binary
seven times (one per fixture), even though the fixture is only a runtime
argument.

Add a new build-reldebinfo job that:
- Configures with RelWithDebInfo, BUILD_BENCHMARKS=ON, BUILD_EXAMPLES=ON,
  ENABLE_WERROR=ON, B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF, and VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR.
- Builds all targets in one pass.
- Uploads bench_callgrind, bench_assembly_navigator, and exampleB4c as
  the reldebinfo-binaries artifact (retention 1 day).

All three callgrind jobs now declare needs: [build-reldebinfo], download
the artifact, and run only the profile + annotate steps inside eic_xl.

Also add -DB4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF to build-test-benchmark. This flag
only affects exampleB4c behavior and is safe to set globally.

bench: add NIST CTC timing benchmarks and point cloud generation (#292)

Replace the unconditional `continue` for IsImportedSelfComparisonFixture
(NIST CTC) with reduced-ray-count BM_rays and BM_inside benchmarks.
Using kNistCtcRayCount=64 rays avoids timeouts on large AP203 compound
assemblies while producing timing data and point cloud output.

mismatches are always 0.  The benchmarks wrap CompareFixtureRays in a
try-catch so a G4Exception on a pathological CTC geometry does not abort
the entire benchmark run.

fix: force TERM=dumb for gh auth PTY, extend timeout to 60s

gh auth login uses bubbletea TUI which emits cursor-positioning
ANSI sequences. With TERM set to a capable value, the fancy UI
draws over itself with escape codes that our sanitizer can't
reliably reconstruct into recognisable strings like
'Login with a web browser'. Setting TERM=dumb + NO_COLOR=1 forces
gh into plain-text fallback mode so the prompt text appears as
literal printable characters.

Also:
- Extend code wait deadline from 30s to 60s
- Log PTY OSErrors at WARNING instead of silently swallowing them
- Dump the sanitised PTY buffer in the timeout warning so failures
  are visible without needing DEBUG logging enabled
- Broaden Stage-1 match: 'web browser' (case-insensitive) in
  addition to exact phrase matches
- Add per-iteration DEBUG log of PTY content for future diagnosis

feat: implement real Copilot API call in /api/ask (0.1.1)

Replace the hardcoded stub response with an actual call to the
GitHub Copilot chat completions API (api.githubcopilot.com) —
the same API that powers the GitHub Copilot CLI.

How it works:
1. The stored GitHub OAuth token (from the auth flow) is exchanged
   for a short-lived Copilot API token via
   api.github.com/copilot_internal/v2/token (cached, auto-refreshed).

   api.githubcopilot.com/chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible).
3. If a Home Assistant MCP server URL is configured, it is included
   in the system prompt so the model knows about it.
4. The real response text is returned to HA Assistant/Assist.

commit.message:
fix: switch gh auth to device code flow (remove --web)

gh auth login --web forces a PKCE web flow with a localhost redirect
that can never complete inside a headless container. Switch to the
standard interactive device code flow used by GitHub Copilot CLI:

  gh auth login --hostname <host> --git-protocol https ...

The PTY driver now uses a two-stage state machine:
  Stage 1 - detect the 'How would you like to authenticate?' prompt
            and press Enter to select 'Login with a web browser'
  Stage 2 - wait for the device code (XXXX-XXXX), capture it, then
            press Enter so gh opens https://github.com/login/device

The user navigates to github.com/login/device, enters the code, and
gh polls for completion — no localhost redirect needed.

Bump to 0.1.0.6.

fix: clear stale pending auth state with null user_code

Three places were preventing the auth code from ever appearing:

1. Bridge startup: if the persisted auth state file contained a
   pending_device_flow with user_code=null (left from a previous
   crashed/timed-out attempt), it was restored and surfaced to HA
   as 'already pending'. Now cleared on startup.

2. Bridge /auth/device/start: if the gh process was running but
   produced no code, the endpoint returned the null-code state
   unchanged instead of restarting. Now kills and restarts.

3. config_flow: a pending state with user_code=None was treated as
   a valid 'already pending' flow, so /auth/device/start was never
   called. Now only reuses a pending state that has a real code.

Bump to 0.1.0.5.

fix: wait for one-time code before pressing Enter in gh auth login

The gh auth login --web flow prints the one-time device code first,
then waits for Enter before opening the browser. Previously, Enter was
sent immediately after spawning the process, before any output was
read. This caused the code to never be captured, so the HA UI showed
no code or verification URL.

Fix: poll gh output for up to 20 seconds waiting for the code to
appear. Once captured, send Enter so gh proceeds with the auth flow.
If no code appears within the timeout, send Enter anyway and let
subsequent polls pick it up.

Also bump versions to 0.1.0.4.

commit.message:
feat: fix constitution loading + improve soul.md personality

Problem:
- OnConversationStart trigger doesn't fire in M365 Copilot and only fires
  once in Teams (session reuse), so the agent's personality/constitution
  from SharePoint (soul.md, user.md, agents.md, tools.md) was never loaded
  in interactive conversations. The agent responded with generic canned text.
- The Greeting topic intercepted 'hey/hi/hello' with a hardcoded response
  and CancelAllDialogs, completely bypassing the AI orchestrator.

Fix (interactive mode only — HeartbeatFlow loads constitution independently):
- New ConversationInit topic: uses OnActivity(type:Message) trigger with
  IsBlank(Global.SoulText) JIT guard. Fires on the first real user message
  in every channel (Teams, M365 Copilot, web chat). Calls GetContext flow
  to populate Global.SoulText/UserText/AgentsText/ToolsText, then lets the
  AI orchestrator handle the response with full personality context.
- Simplified ConversationStart: removed GetContext call (now in ConversationInit),
  kept minimal greeting for channels where OnConversationStart does fire.
- Deleted Greeting topic: no more canned responses — the orchestrator handles
  greetings naturally using the loaded constitution.
- Improved soul.md template in build-bootstrap-flow.py: rewrote from a
  compliance-style bullet list to a conversational personality definition.
  Added 'Relationship to the User' section that explicitly instructs the
  agent to use context from user.md (name, preferences, etc.) in responses.
  Produces warmer, more personalized interactions vs generic introductions.

commit.message:
Fix door migration: cells now actually pass through doors

Root cause: bounceInRoom() was bouncing cells away from door edges,
and the 0.3% random migration chance was far too low.

New approach:
- bounceInRoom() skips Y-boundary bounce when cell is horizontally
  inside a doorway opening (isInDoorZoneX() helper)
- checkDoorMigration() detects when cell has entered the gap zone
  (the physical space between floors) and teleports it deterministically
  — no random chance needed, cells just drift through naturally

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix door rendering and cell migration coordinate bugs

- drawDoors: use di.y (not di.y + FLOOR_H) for gap rect and frame lines
- checkDoorMigration: fix nearDoorEdge — lowerFloor cells trigger at room TOP,
  upperFloor cells trigger at room BOTTOM (matches physical layout)
- checkDoorMigration: fix stale this.floorNum bug in teleport Y calculation;
  save originalFloor before updating roomId/floorNum
- Fix migration direction: going up -> appear at bottom of upper room,
  going down -> appear at top of lower room (adjacent to door gap)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: p5js-living-rooms cellular life simulation

Three-floor house simulation where cellular life (Producers, Herbivores,
Predators, Decomposers) reacts to Home Assistant environmental metrics
(temperature, humidity, light, motion, CO2). Cells migrate between floors
through doors. Data loaded from rooms.json and refreshed periodically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
